pages.full_text:
way to Thorpeness. We had lunch in a field; Eve had bought tip-over yoghurts, & most of the children tipped the yoghurt over themselves while tipping the flavour into the yoghurt.
	At Annette's Eve made tea, & then we went for a walk with Penel & the 2 dogs, Lucy & Pippa. Very energetic, romping puppies. We met Charles Henneker on the way. His son, Penel's godson, Thomas, 9, was spending the night with Penel. Thomas slept with Penel & Thomas, while Laura, William & Peter slept in the house & John & Charles & Eve were in the van. It rained in the night, but was clear next day. It was quite a squash getting up onto the flat bit of Annette's drive. Thomas & Annette had to hold the tree branches back. Annette cooked us a very good barbeque – she had started the charcoal
pages.notes: Page continues from previous spread (text starts mid-sentence with 'way to Thorpeness.'). No handwritten page number visible in top-left corner. Text ends mid-sentence 'she had started the charcoal', continued on right page. Year cannot be determined from this page alone — no year marker present. Dogs named Lucy and Pippa; people named Annette, Penel, Charles Henneker, Thomas, Laura, William, Peter, John, Charles, Eve.

pages.full_text:
in a jug.

__Sunday 16th August__
	Annette gave us breakfast & we went to church at Eye. Lovely high church. We had to return to Thornham Magna because Thomas had forgotten his cap.
	We reached __Thorpeness__ in time for lunch.
	Jane feeds us lunch & we have camping van breakfasts & suppers every day. I hope it doesn't rain.

__Sunday 24th August__
	__Friday__ Yesterday Simon joined us. Today he drove off at 10·15. to take Thomas to Beauchamp. Then Simon drove home to B'ham. Everyone else went home in the rela[illegible]

__Friday 29th August__
	Simon, Eve, [strikethrough: Laura], William, Peter, John, Charles went down for the Beauchamp
pages.notes: Handwritten page number 136 in top-right corner. Page begins mid-sentence 'in a jug.' (continuation from left page). 'Eye' is a town in Suffolk; 'Thornham Magna' is a village in Suffolk; 'Thorpeness' is a village in Suffolk; 'Beauchamp' likely refers to a place or house name (possibly Beauchamp in the Midlands area given 'B'ham' = Birmingham). Under 'Sunday 24th August', the word 'Friday' appears underlined then the text continues — possibly a correction or annotation. 'Laura' appears struck through in the Friday 29th entry. The word after 'rela' is cut off or illegible at edge of page. Year cannot be determined from this page — no year marker. The three August dates (16th Sunday, 24th Sunday, 29th Friday) are consistent with calendar years: 1992 (16 Aug Sun, 24 Aug Mon — doesn't fit), 1998 (16 Aug Sun, 24 Aug Mon — doesn't fit), 2003 (16 Aug Sat — doesn't fit); no single year has all three matching exactly, so year is not asserted.
